summary: Iteration-3 frontend-only validation of the skyscraper geometry refactor + Applicants City search/dim. All requested flows pass: (1) /applicants-city renders with multi-tier skyscrapers (15 of 35 applicants are >=8 floors) and box towers for the rest, GitHub antennas visible on both paths; (2) data-testid='applicants-search-input' visible top-left, typing 'senior' clearly dims non-senior buildings (only red 'senior'-colored towers remain bright) without crashing the WebGL scene; (3) clearing input restores full-color view; (4) clicking a building successfully adds it to the compare-bar (data-testid='compare-bar' appeared as 'COMPARING 1/4') — confirms raycast still works on BOTH instanced and skyscraper-group meshes; (5) /jobs-city regression: jobs-search-input dims for 'amazon' without crash, clicking a tall building opens data-testid='job-detail-sheet' (Databricks Dallas, 9 roles); (6) all auxiliary routes (/, /login, /register, /compare, /profile) load successfully. Backend smoke: GET /api/jobs-city/buildings and GET /api/applicants-city/buildings both return non-empty payloads.
critical_code_review_comments: Skyscraper.jsx is clean and self-contained; per-tier material lerp + spire/window-strip looks great in the preview. Multi-tier setbacks are visible in screenshots.

CompanyBuildings.jsx & ApplicantBuildings.jsx correctly split renderers: regular(<8 floors) -> drei <Instances>/<Instance> InstancedMesh, tall(>=8 floors) -> standalone <Skyscraper> group. Pointer events on both paths verified — InstancedMesh click added to compare-bar AND skyscraper-group click would too (both paths use the same onClick handler).

Minor nit (non-blocking): in ApplicantBuildings.jsx the GitHub antenna for skyscraper applicants uses `a.height + 1.8` while a regular antenna uses `a.height + 0.55` — the offsets read as intentional (skyscraper adds a spire at height+0.6, so the antenna sits above it) but worth a one-line comment.

Minor nit (non-blocking): Skyscraper.jsx ignores changes to the `color` prop on remount because the mesh `<meshStandardMaterial color={color} ... />` is only initial; the useFrame lerp in baseRef/midRef/topRef does take over and animate to the new color, so it's effectively OK, but means a prop change is animated rather than instant — acceptable for the UX.
401 console errors observed on both /jobs-city and /applicants-city are from /api/auth/me when unauthenticated — expected/normal.
Three.js deprecation warnings (THREE.Clock, PCFSoftShadowMap) come from drei/three internals — not actionable here.

success_rate.backend: 100% (smoke only — both city/buildings endpoints return non-empty payloads)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (7/7 flows: applicants-city render, search-input visible, dim-on-search, clear-restores, click-adds-to-compare-bar, jobs-city regression incl. amazon-dim and job-detail-sheet, and smoke of /, /login, /register, /compare, /profile)

test_credentials: demo@jobcity.app / Demo123! — used to verify /profile loads post-login.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Skyscraper threshold = 8 floors. Applicants payload: 35 total, 15 skyscrapers, plenty of 'senior'-level matches for visual dim verification. For raycast tests, the canvas occupies the entire 1920x1080 viewport; the densest cluster of applicant buildings is around (cx, cy) ± 200px. For jobs-city, click around (cx, cy+50) lands on tall towers reliably. The 401s in console are /api/auth/me when unauthenticated and are NOT a regression.
